page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton2 processors built on the 64-bit ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and it operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU scoring 97024.22 and Geekbench software compilation with Clang reaching 43397.0. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is highly efficient at 3782.37 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average tier at 166.94 GB/sec and 74.44 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the lower tier. However, the server excels in high-throughput workloads, demonstrating strong performance in Redis SET operations at 73.0 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 45.02 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation provide high resource density and compute throughput per unit of power, making it a viable alternative to x86 platforms for parallelizable tasks. The combination of high-speed local NVMe storage and strong multi-core capabilities makes this server well-suited for high-performance web serving, distributed databases, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is less optimal for single-threaded legacy applications or large-scale LLM inference tasks, which show weaker benchmark results.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton2 processor using the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 64 dedicated physical cores at 2.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of RAM, a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput for Redis operations and static web serving. However, single-core execution and single-threaded decompression represent performance tradeoffs. This server offers a resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for parallel workloads, making it highly suitable for multi-threaded software compilation, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 128 GB RAM, a 3800 GB local NVMe SSD, and strong multi-core and Redis performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 3800 GB NVMe SSD for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for parallelized, high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 3800 GB local NVMe SSD
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput Redis operations
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for multi-threaded software compilation and static web serving
